reason: The text describes the impending destruction of Jerusalem by Babylon as divine judgment ('because of the anger of Yahweh'). This encompasses a significant, comprehensive disaster affecting the general public: siege warfare, famine, burning of the Temple and royal buildings, demolition of city walls, widespread exile of the population, and execution of leaders. The destruction is explicitly both man-made (Babylonian military action under Nebuchadnezzar) and supernatural (attributed to Yahweh's judgment and anger), and impacts the entire city and populace rather than isolated individuals.
